python - <<'PY'
import hashlib
import os
import urllib.request
from pathlib import Path

repo_id = 'knightnemo/mlsbench-jepa-planning-ac-jepa-two-rooms'
filename = 'e-11.pth.tar'
expected_sha256 = 'f5d015b66745d1de5352cf03dfe2fd537889d6f101e4e84bc50b9c17bca45abf'
out_dir = Path('/data/eb_jepa/checkpoints')
out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
out_path = out_dir / 'ac_jepa_e11.pth.tar'

def sha256sum(path: Path) -> str:
    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    with path.open('rb') as fh:
        for chunk in iter(lambda: fh.read(1024 * 1024), b''):
            digest.update(chunk)
    return digest.hexdigest()

if out_path.exists() and sha256sum(out_path) == expected_sha256:
    print(f'Checkpoint already present: {out_path}')
    raise SystemExit(0)

endpoints = []
if os.environ.get('HF_ENDPOINT'):
    endpoints.append(os.environ['HF_ENDPOINT'].rstrip('/'))
endpoints.extend(['https://hf-mirror.com', 'https://huggingface.co'])
seen = set()
last_error = None
for endpoint in endpoints:
    if endpoint in seen:
        continue
    seen.add(endpoint)
    url = f"{endpoint}/{repo_id}/resolve/main/{filename}"
    print(f'Downloading checkpoint from {url}')
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=120) as response, out_path.open('wb') as fh:
            while True:
                chunk = response.read(1024 * 1024)
                if not chunk:
                    break
                fh.write(chunk)
    except Exception as exc:
        last_error = exc
        print(f'Failed to download from {endpoint}: {exc}')
        out_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
        continue

    digest = sha256sum(out_path)
    if digest == expected_sha256:
        print(f'Checkpoint ready at {out_path}')
        break

    last_error = RuntimeError(f'sha256 mismatch: expected {expected_sha256}, got {digest}')
    print(last_error)
    out_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
else:
    raise RuntimeError(f'Unable to fetch {filename}: {last_error}')
PY

python - <<'PY'
import os, sys, shutil, urllib.request, zipfile
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path('/data/eb_jepa/tiny-imagenet-200')
MARKER = ROOT / '.organized'
if MARKER.exists():
    print(f'Tiny-ImageNet already prepared at {ROOT}')
    raise SystemExit(0)

zip_path = Path('/data/eb_jepa/tiny-imagenet-200.zip')
urls = [
    'http://cs231n.stanford.edu/tiny-imagenet-200.zip',
    'https://image-net.org/data/tiny-imagenet-200.zip',
]
if not zip_path.exists():
    last = None
    for url in urls:
        try:
            print(f'Downloading Tiny-ImageNet from {url}')
            with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=600) as r, zip_path.open('wb') as fh:
                shutil.copyfileobj(r, fh, length=1024 * 1024)
            break
        except Exception as e:
            last = e
            print(f'  failed: {e}')
            zip_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
    else:
        raise RuntimeError(f'All Tiny-ImageNet mirrors failed: {last}')
print(f'zip size: {zip_path.stat().st_size/1e6:.1f} MB')

# Unzip into /data/eb_jepa/
print('Unzipping...')
with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, 'r') as zf:
    zf.extractall('/data/eb_jepa/')
print('Unzip complete')

# Re-organize val/ to ImageFolder format using val_annotations.txt
val_dir = ROOT / 'val'
images_dir = val_dir / 'images'
ann_file = val_dir / 'val_annotations.txt'
assert ann_file.exists(), f'missing {ann_file}'
img_to_class = {}
for line in ann_file.read_text().splitlines():
    parts = line.split('\t')
    if len(parts) >= 2:
        img_to_class[parts[0]] = parts[1]
print(f'Re-organizing {len(img_to_class)} val images by class')
for img_name, wnid in img_to_class.items():
    src = images_dir / img_name
    if not src.exists():
        continue
    dst_dir = val_dir / wnid / 'images'
    dst_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    shutil.move(str(src), str(dst_dir / img_name))
# remove now-empty images/ dir
try:
    images_dir.rmdir()
except OSError:
    pass

# Sanity check class counts
train_classes = sorted(p.name for p in (ROOT / 'train').iterdir() if p.is_dir())
val_classes = sorted(p.name for p in val_dir.iterdir() if p.is_dir() and p.name != 'images')
assert len(train_classes) == 200, f'train has {len(train_classes)} classes'
assert len(val_classes) == 200, f'val has {len(val_classes)} classes'
assert train_classes == val_classes, 'train/val class mismatch'
print(f'Tiny-ImageNet ready: 200 classes, train={sum(1 for _ in (ROOT/"train").rglob("*.JPEG"))}, val={sum(1 for _ in val_dir.rglob("*.JPEG"))}')

# delete zip to save space
zip_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
MARKER.write_text('ok\n')
print(f'Marker written: {MARKER}')
PY
